I found the **UsersController.scala** file. Here's the User data model:

## User Data Model

The `User` is defined as a **case class** on line 4:

```scala
case class User(id: Int, name: String, email: String, createdAt: LocalDateTime)
```

### Fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `Int` | The user's unique identifier |
| `name` | `String` | The user's name |
| `email` | `String` | The user's email address |
| `createdAt` | `LocalDateTime` | Timestamp when the user was created |

The file also shows example usage with sample users:
- User 1: Alice (alice@example.com, created 2024-01-15 10:30)
- User 2: Bob (bob@example.com, created 2024-02-20 14:00)
- User 3: Charlie (charlie@example.com, created 2024-03-05 09:15)
